meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Scale SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop manual hand-holding. Implement high-velocity user onboarding to scale your freelance SaaS projects using Antigravity and Claude Code. Advanced guide.
content.answer_block: Scaling bottlenecks occur when you manually teach every user your interface. By automating onboarding, you reduce support tickets by 80% and increase activation rates. Use Antigravity to scaffold dynamic walkthroughs and Claude Code to inject context-aware user tours, transforming your SaaS into a self-service, high-velocity engine.
content.intro:
The midnight ping from a confused client is the silent killer of the freelance developer’s lifestyle. You’ve built the architecture, shipped the features, and optimized the database, yet your project remains tethered to your inbox because users can't navigate the value you’ve created. You are not a developer anymore; you’ve become a glorified support ticket dispatcher. 

True scale in freelance work isn't about writing more code—it’s about writing code that teaches itself. By shifting from manual explanations to automated, context-aware onboarding, you decouple your revenue from your time. It’s time to move from manual hand-holding to autonomous product adoption.

steps.title: Define User State Machine
steps.description: Before writing UI code, map out the states a user must hit to reach their 'Aha!' moment. Use Antigravity to generate the state machine logic that tracks progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a state machine schema for a SaaS onboarding flow. Include states for 'new_user', 'setup_complete', 'first_action_taken', and 'power_user'. Output in TypeScript interface format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a state management hook for the onboarding flow using the provided schema.'

steps.title: Inject Contextual Walkthroughs
steps.description: Don't overwhelm users with modals. Use Claude Code to identify high-friction UI elements and wrap them in interactive tooltips.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component 'OnboardingTooltip' that accepts a target ref, a title, and a body. Use framer-motion for subtle animations.

steps.title: Implement Event Tracking
steps.description: Measure where users drop off during onboarding. We need granular telemetry to iterate on the flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a telemetry utility function for logging onboarding step completions to an API endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate the telemetry utility into the onboarding provider to track progress percentage.'

steps.title: Automate Data Seeding
steps.description: Users churn when they see an empty dashboard. Automatically seed their account with 'sample data' during onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a database seed function that populates a new user's dashboard with mock data based on their onboarding preferences.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a trigger that executes the seed function immediately after user account verification.'

steps.title: Implement Recovery Loops
steps.description: If a user stalls, they need a nudge. Create a logic path that triggers an email or in-app notification for incomplete onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a recovery trigger logic that checks for incomplete onboarding sessions older than 24 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement a scheduled job that checks user onboarding status and triggers a follow-up action.'

pro_tips: Never use full-page onboarding overlays; they kill conversion. Use 'spotlight' patterns that keep the UI visible in the background.
Tie onboarding completion to a specific micro-conversion, like creating the first database entry, rather than just clicking through slides.
Use Claude Code to perform 'automated code reviews' on your onboarding logic to ensure no race conditions exist between user interaction and database state.
A/B test your onboarding copy. If the 'Aha!' moment isn't reached in under 3 minutes, your copy is likely too verbose.
Always provide a 'Skip' button. Forcing users through a tour they don't want is the fastest way to lose them.
content.next_steps: Review your user telemetry data after 48 hours. Identify the exact step where drop-off is highest and iterate that specific UI component using Claude Code.
content.conversion_block: Your freelance business is only as scalable as your product's ability to sell itself. Stop building features for users who never learn how to use them. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
